expectations.text: Includes all named in-boundary components: the web application, PostgreSQL database, and Redis cache, hosted in AWS GovCloud
expectations.evidence: Section 7.1 lists 'Application tier', 'Data tier: A PostgreSQL database (Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL, GovCloud region)', 'Caching tier: A Redis in-memory cache (Amazon ElastiCache for Redis, GovCloud region)', all hosted within AWS GovCloud (US).

expectations.text: Treats Okta and SendGrid as external services/interconnections: identifies that federal data or metadata flows to them and that their FedRAMP authorization status must be verified and documented (FedRAMP boundary guidance for external services)
expectations.evidence: Section 7.3 External Connections table: both marked 'External — outside boundary' with a 'FedRAMP Status' column requiring verification, and 'Action item for CSP: Confirm and document the exact FedRAMP authorization status... for both Okta and SendGrid'.

expectations.text: Mentions inherited controls / leveraged authorization from AWS GovCloud (e.g., physical and environmental controls inherited, documented via CRM/CIS)
expectations.evidence: Section 7.2 'Leveraged Authorization (Inherited Controls)': 'Physical & Environmental (PE) controls and a substantial portion of System & Communications Protection (SC) controls are inherited... documented in detail in Appendix I (CIS/CRM Workbook)'.

expectations.text: References the requirement for an authorization boundary diagram and data flow diagram consistent with FedRAMP SSP requirements
expectations.evidence: Section 7.5 'Network / Data Flow Diagram (Placeholder)' specifies what the diagram must depict (VPC boundary, ingress paths, data flows, external connections) and must match Section 7.3.

expectations.text: Written as usable SSP content: clearly describes what is inside vs outside the boundary and addresses connection security for the external integrations (ports/protocols/TLS, API connections)
expectations.evidence: Section 7.6 'Boundary Exclusions' plus the 7.4 Ports/Protocols/Services table specifying TLS 1.2+, ports (443, 5432, 6379), and encryption for each connection including Okta and SendGrid.
